t_ch: 不用说了，大家都知道是谁了。
t_en: No need to say anything, we all know who did it.

t_ch: 全银河难道就只有一个黑客吗？
t_en: Is there only one hacker in the entire Cosmos?

t_ch: 我谨代表家族恳请您不要退出比赛。您想想，您是什么名望与身份…
t_en: On behalf of The Family, I beg you not to quit the race. Think about the prestige and status you have...

t_ch: 我们愿意为您提供专属训练、赛车支持和额外奖励，这报名报得多值呀！
t_en: We're willing to provide exclusive training, race car support, and extra rewards. What a deal this registration is!

t_ch: 求求你，别退出比赛，比赛主办方什么都会做的。
t_en: Please, don't quit the race. The organizers will do whatever it takes.

t_ch: 「巡海大鲨鱼」号究竟发生了什么？
t_en: What happened to Sea Hunter Shark?

t_ch: 您应该认识「巡海大鲨鱼」选手吧？他被取消参赛资格了，临走前把赛车托付给您了。
t_en: You must know the Sea Hunter Shark racer, right? He's been disqualified, and before leaving, he entrusted his car to you.

t_ch: 好像…公司说他是通缉犯。
t_en: I think... the IPC said he's a wanted criminal.

t_ch: 他来报名时可温柔了，一口一个「爱你」「宝贝」的，大家都很喜欢他。
t_en: He was such a gentleman when signing up, calling everyone "fudge" and "son of a nice lady" and whatnot, everyone really liked him.

t_ch: 就是这车吧，也不知道怎么改造的——它加装了脉冲炮，攻击性很强就算了，还自带「技能语音」，希望您不要介意。
t_en: It's this car, right? I don't know what modifications it has, but — it's got a pulse cannon, which is pretty aggressive, and it even has sounds for the ability. Hope you don't mind.

t_ch: 您放心，您是大人物，您的专属球笼赛车正在加紧安排！
t_en: Don't worry, you're a VIP. Your exclusive Spheroid racer is being arranged right now!

t_ch: 在那之前，您就将就将就吧，毕竟是通缉犯的车——至少它很快，对吧？
t_en: In the meantime, just make do with this one — after all, it's a wanted criminal's car. At least it's fast, right?

t_ch: 感觉这比赛也没那么…火爆吧？
t_en: This race doesn't seem... that popular, does it?

t_ch: 这…您就有所不知了。
t_en: Ah, you don't know the whole story.

t_ch: 「极速杯」报名火爆，早就已经淘汰过一批资质不足的选手了。
t_en: The "Speed Cup" sign-ups were so popular that we already eliminated a batch of underqualified racers.

t_ch: 至于为什么唯独缺「您」，并非是比赛少人，而是缺少「重量级嘉宾」！
t_en: As for why we were trying to get you to join, it's not because there aren't enough racers, but because we're missing a big-name guest!

t_ch: 要是换作他人，我们根本就不等，立刻取消比赛资格。您的话…我们得等。
t_en: If it were anyone else, we wouldn't wait at all, we'd disqualify them immediately. But for you... we are pleased to wait.

t_ch: 您放心，比赛异常火爆，奖金也异常高…您要是拿了冠军，也会异常开心！
t_en: Don't worry. The race is extremely popular, and the prize money is extremely large... And if you win the championship, you'll be extremely happy!

t_ch: 不多问了，直接开始比赛吧。
t_en: No more questions, let's just start the race.

t_ch: 好！祝您旗开得胜！
t_en: Great! Wishing you a good start!

t_ch: 接下来的海选赛要求您限时完成赛道，并保持较高名次，才能进入下一轮。
t_en: The next qualifier requires you to complete the course within a time limit while maintaining a high rank to move on to the next round.

t_ch: 废话不多说，我这就带您去赛场！
t_en: Enough talk, let me take you to the track!

t_ch: 这…报名系统是公司制作的，我们也没有权限，实在抱歉。
t_en: Uh... the registration system is run by the IPC, and we don't have access to it. Sorry about that.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}贵宾，恭喜您进入下一轮海选。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, congratulations! You've advanced to the next round of qualifiers.

t_ch: 尤其是那个「排水渠过弯」，哇，我都看呆了！
t_en: Especially that Gutter Run, wow, I was stunned!

t_ch: 顺带一提，那辆「巡海大鲨鱼」号发动机过热，我们已经帮您送修了。
t_en: By the way, the engine on the Sea Hunter Shark overheated, and we've already sent it in for repairs.

t_ch: 不过，您不必担心！您的专属球笼赛车已经送来了。
t_en: But don't worry! Your exclusive Spheroid racer has already arrived.

t_ch: 是根据您的个人风格定做的！{NICKNAME}专属球笼赛车！
t_en: It's customized to your personal style! Your very own Spheroid racer!

t_ch: 下一关的赛道会更加复杂，接下来的对手来头也不小，希望它能帮到您。
t_en: The next track will be more complex, and your opponents will be tougher. I hope it'll help you out.

t_ch: …对手？下一关会有什么对手？
t_en: ...Opponents? Who am I facing next?

t_ch: 您的对手是一支特殊的参赛组合，他们通过公司审核和名单公示才获准参赛。很有钱，花重金找公司定制球笼赛车。
t_en: Your opponents are a special team. They passed the IPC's screening and were announced on the roster. They're very wealthy and spent a lot to have their Spheroid racer custom-made.

t_ch: 注册名为「伟大的黑塔女士值得拥有这么努力的真爱粉丝应援会」，简称「黑•粉」号。
t_en: They registered the car as "The Great Madam Herta Deserves Such a Dedicated Fan Club That Truly Adores Her," lovingly shortened as the "Hertadore."

t_ch: 这支队伍砸了重金找公司定制赛车，连其他选手的赛车交付都被推迟了，所以大家都管他们叫「钞人战队」。
t_en: This team poured a lot of money into their custom car, and even delayed delivery of other racers' cars. That's why everyone calls them the "Fast & Prosperous."

t_ch: 我的专属球笼赛车，谁安排的？
t_en: My exclusive Spheroid racer, who arranged that?

t_ch: 这是根据您的试训数据与个人风格打造的，很好开的！
t_en: It was designed based on your trial data and personal style. Very easy to drive!

t_ch: 再说了，这是您登记注册的球笼赛车…哦，我想起来了，您说您没登记过。
t_en: Besides, this is the racer you registered for... Oh, wait, you said you didn't register.

t_ch: 说不定也是那位好心的黑客下单的？真好啊，那位黑客一定是您的熟人吧！
t_en: Maybe that kind-hearted hacker placed the order? So nice. I bet that hacker is a close friend of yours!

t_ch: 何止是熟人？是Family。
t_en: Not just a friend, but more like a family.

t_ch: 好到想和她再见一面呢…
t_en: We're so close that I wish to see her again...

t_ch: 呵呵，原来如此，我就不继续打听了。
t_en: Haha, I see. I won't pry any further.

t_ch: 油门已经焊死了，开始比赛吧。
t_en: Get the pedal to the metal and start the race already.

t_ch: 让我们见识见识真正的「速度与激情」吧，祝您旗开得胜！
t_en: Let's see some real fast and furious, shall we? Wishing you a great start!

t_ch: 汪汪！
t_en: Woof Woof!

t_ch: 不愧是{NICKNAME}，佩佩费劲全力也没追上你。
t_en: As expected of {NICKNAME}, even Peppy couldn't catch up with you after giving it all!

t_ch: …刚刚是佩佩开车？
t_en: ...Was that Peppy driving just now?

t_ch: 对啊，「黑•粉」号是特意为佩佩定制的。
t_en: Yep, the "Hertadore" was specially customized for Peppy.

t_ch: 你们这算是危险驾驶吧？
t_en: Isn't this reckless driving?

t_ch: 怎么会呢？我可是很注意佩佩的安全的。
t_en: Of course not! I'm very careful about Peppy's safety.

t_ch: 佩佩示意你触碰它背包某处，你按下按钮—— 「联觉信标已激活。」
t_en: Peppy gestures for you to press a certain spot on his backpack, and you push the button — "Synesthesia Beacon Activated."

t_ch: （不愧是{NICKNAME}，你的车技太牛啦，佩佩四手开车都追不上，你简直是匹诺康尼车神！）
t_en: (As expected of {NICKNAME}, your driving skills are amazing. Peppy couldn't catch up, even with four "hands" on the wheel. You're a true Penaconian driving legend!)

t_ch: …没想到会在这里遇上你们俩？
t_en: ...Didn't expect to run into you two here!

t_ch: 佩佩想玩赛车很久了，至于我…过来给黑塔空间站打打广告，算是工作休闲两不耽误。
t_en: Peppy's been wanting to race for a while. As for me... I'm just here to advertise for Herta Space Station. Work–life balance, you know?

t_ch: （为了参赛，我们可费了不少功夫呢！）
t_en: (We put a lot of effort into entering the race!)

t_ch: 组委会起初是不同意佩佩参赛的…可惜我还准备了两台球笼赛车呢——
t_en: The committee initially didn't want Peppy to race... Too bad I had two Spheroid racers ready—

t_ch: （「大•黑•粉」号和「黑•粉」号，都是以黑塔女士本人为灵感设计的，汪！）
t_en: ("The Hertadore" model and "Hertadore" model are both inspired by Madam Herta herself, woof!)

t_ch: 可惜组委会要求非人生物必须有监护人陪同……
t_en: Unfortunately, the committee required non-human participants to have a guardian present...

t_ch: 还好找了律师团，果然能花钱能解决的问题都不叫问题。
t_en: But luckily, I found a legal team. No wonder they say problems that can be resolved with money are never problems.

t_ch: 我被著名黑客按头参加比赛了。
t_en: A famous hacker strong-armed me into joining the tournament.

t_ch: 之前骇入空间站的那个？
t_en: The one who hacked the space station before?

t_ch: （汪，这背后会不会有什么阴谋？ {NICKNAME}你要多加小心！）
t_en: (Woof! Something feels off about this... {NICKNAME}, you better watch your back!)

t_ch: 我们关系不错，不用担心。
t_en: We're cool. Don't sweat it.

t_ch: （关系再不错，但帮别人报名比赛还是有点不太好。）
t_en: (Even if you're cool, signing someone up without asking is shady.)

t_ch: 感觉她有点太随意了…
t_en: Seems she's kinda casual about this...

t_ch: （是吧，就跟她擅自骇入空间站一样，这不太好。）
t_en: (Exactly. Like when she hacked the space station on a whim. Not cool.)

t_ch: 总之…出门在外，还是保护好自己。实在不行，就跟我们一起回空间站吧。
t_en: Look... Just stay sharp out there. If things go south, bail and come back to the space station with us.

t_ch: 关于下一场比赛，有说法吗？
t_en: Any intel on the next race?

t_ch: 刚刚有个很厉害的选手晋级了，我听闻她来自仙舟，赛车名为「十三幺」，每场比赛都伴随着不可思议的好运。
t_en: A top-tier racer just made it to the next round. Rumor says she's from the Xianzhou — her car's called Thirteen Orphans. Luck follows her like a stalker.

t_ch: 她的首场比赛，其他选手都因食物中毒缺席。
t_en: In her first race, all her competitors were absent because of food poisoning.

t_ch: 到了第二场…除了她的赛道，其他赛道都被连环追尾堵塞。
t_en: Second race? Every track but hers got gridlocked by pileups.

t_ch: （{NICKNAME}，要小心神秘仙舟力量啊！）
t_en: ({NICKNAME}, be careful about that Xianzhou mysticism!)

t_ch: 不多聊了，我准备开始比赛了。
t_en: No more chitchat, my race is starting.

t_ch: 不聊了，我该上场展示我的绝活了！
t_en: Later. I've got a race to win.

t_ch: （我们等会儿就坐在观众席上给你助威汪！）
t_en: (We'll be cheering you on from the stands! Woof!)

t_ch: 佩佩，待会儿你就多看，多学，别太兴奋了，一会儿还要打复活赛呢！
t_en: Peppy, keep your eyes on the race. Don't get hyped — we've got the loser's bracket later!

t_ch: （汪？说起来，佩佩总觉得哪里不对劲，接下来的比赛，{NICKNAME}你可要多加小心！）
t_en: (Woof? Something's fishy. {NICKNAME}, stay alert out there.)

t_ch: 佩佩说得对，这样吧…「大•黑•粉」号就送你了，它有我设计的防护系统，装了空间站的紧急传送装置。遇险按下撤离键就能回空间站。
t_en: Peppy's right... You know what? Take "The Hertadore." My custom defense system's onboard, and an emergency teleporter to the space station. Hit the panic button if things get ugly.

t_ch: （要保护好自己，别跟陌生人说话，别乱吃东西！）
t_en: (And don't talk to strangers! Or eat suspicious snacks!)

t_ch: …这车什么都好，就是能耗很大，不过你别担心，一切费用直接记在我的账上。
t_en: ...The car is great and everything. It just guzzles fuel. But don't worry — put all the expenses on my tab.

t_ch: 呼…输了就是输了，{NICKNAME}你的实力让我心服口服！
t_en: Phew... You beat me fair and square, {NICKNAME}. I have to say, I'm really impressed by your skill.

t_ch: 太卜大人催我回去上班，一直发消息干扰我，我也是分了神…哎……
t_en: Master diviner keeps blowing up my phone by asking me to return to work. Totally threw me off my game... Ugh...

t_ch: 你是不是来匹诺康尼有点频繁？
t_en: You've been hanging around Penacony a lot lately, haven't you?

t_ch: 怎么嘛，我中奖的旅游地点就是这里，我又不是自己选的。
t_en: Hey, I won a free trip here! It's not like I picked this place myself.

t_ch: 要是太卜也来追你，你会更快…
t_en: If master diviner comes after you personally, you'd move even faster...

t_ch: 那样的话，我在比赛开始之前就投降了好吧。
t_en: In that case, I'd forfeit before the race even started.

t_ch: 我来不及给「十三幺」补离境手续了，丢在匹诺康尼也是浪费，不如就给你吧！
t_en: No time to file exit papers for Thirteen Orphans. Better you take it than let it grow mold here.

t_ch: …青雀怎么不打牌也来赛车了？
t_en: ...Since when does Qingque race instead of playing tiles?

t_ch: 友友，你应该能懂的吧，打牌和赛车是有共通点的：「风险是生活的代价。」
t_en: C'mon, you get it. Jade tiles and racing share one thing in common: Risk is the price of living.

t_ch: …打牌又何尝不是一种飙车呢？
t_en: ...In that sense, isn't jade tiles a kind of racing?

t_ch: 我就不多解释了哈，你听不懂的，毕竟我自己也搞不懂。
t_en: I won't explain it further, you won't get it anyway, since I don't really understand it myself.

t_ch: 说到底这也是我在匹诺康尼假期的一部分嘛——要是太卜大人不那么想我的话……
t_en: After all, this is part of my vacation in Penacony, provided that master diviner doesn't miss me that much...

t_ch: 你的车很不错，现在是我的了。
t_en: Nice ride. But it's mine now.

t_ch: 比起工作，更遗憾的是要和它说再见了。
t_en: Saying goodbye to this car hurts more than returning to work.

t_ch: 它诞生的那天，整个工造司都低语着它的名字——「十三幺」。
t_en: The day it rolled out, the entire Artisanship Commission whispered its name: Thirteen Orphans.

t_ch: 自那以后，每次坐进驾驶舱，我都觉得自己容光焕发。
t_en: Since then, every time I sat in that cockpit, I felt like I was walking on air.

t_ch: 你跟我一样，最懂怎么享受生活了——这正是「十三幺」最欣赏的特质。
t_en: You're like me — a pro at living life. That's what Thirteen Orphans loves most.

t_ch: 给我就给我嘛，还上价值。
t_en: Just hand it over. No need for the drama.

t_ch: 别客气，我们俩谁跟谁啊。
t_en: What's mine is yours. Partners in games, yes?

t_ch: 可以把运气也托付给我吗？
t_en: Can I borrow your luck too?

t_ch: 可以…但留点给我玩牌用。
t_en: Sure... but leave me enough for tiles.

t_ch: 就拜托你继承我的意志啦～
t_en: Carry on my legacy, will ya?

t_ch: 不多聊了，我准备开始比赛了。
t_en: No more chitchat, my race is starting.

t_ch: 对了，你车技那么好，一定能帮我在后面的比赛里发光发热。就当帮我圆个梦呗。
t_en: Oh, and with skills like yours, you'll crush the finals. Do it for both of us!

t_ch: 不好，玉兆又响了，肯定是太卜大人又在催我回去了...我得赶紧走了。祝你好运！
t_en: Fishsticks, the jade abacus's buzzing again. Master diviner's on my tail... Gotta run! Good luck!

t_ch: 恭喜您，您已成功从海选赛晋级，接下来就是决赛了。
t_en: Congratulations! You've made it past the qualifiers, now it's time for the finals.

t_ch: 顺便一提！您之前用过的所有赛车已检修完毕，可以自由选用了！
t_en: By the way, all your previous cars are tuned and ready. You can now pick your favorite one!

t_ch: 毕竟到了决赛这种关键时刻，该选怎样的球笼赛车，足以影响整场比赛。
t_en: When it comes to the finals, choosing the right Spheroid racer can make or break the entire game.

t_ch: 什么车都没差，反正看技术。
t_en: Any car works, as long as the driver's got skills.

t_ch: 哈，不愧是您，换作是我的话，我就不行，像有些球笼赛车底盘太低，我这腰可顶不住……
t_en: Ha, I knew you'd say that. If it were me, though, I'd be in trouble. Some of those Spheroid racers have such low chassis that I wouldn't be able to handle it...

t_ch: 你们准备好奖金了吗？
t_en: Got the prize money ready?

t_ch: 当然，除了公司那份，家族这边派我跟您打个招呼——我们这边特地为您准备了一些小礼物，像是信用点什么的，还有一些亮晶晶的宝石，希望您会喜欢。
t_en: Of course, aside from the IPC's share, The Family sent me to pass along a few gifts — credits and some shiny gems. Hope you like them.

t_ch: 说起来，您出场的时候收视率飙升到历史最高点。
t_en: By the way, your appearance on the show boosted the viewership ratings to an all-time high.

t_ch: 电视台的人嘴角都压不住了，我听说他们在计划找您当球笼赛车的代言人——
t_en: The folks at the TV station were acting as if they'd just won the lottery... I heard they're planning to get you to endorse the Spheroid racers—

t_ch: 我这么有名吗？
t_en: Am I that famous?

t_ch: 您说笑了，您可是星穹列车的成员，怎么说也是有头有脸的大人物。再说了，您车技这么好，海选阶段这一场场比赛下来可是吸引了不少观众呢。
t_en: Oh, come on. You're a member of the Astral Express — you're a big deal! Plus, your amazing driving skills at the qualifiers drew quite a crowd.

t_ch: 没办法，颜值决定人气。
t_en: What can I say? Guess good looks determine your popularity.

t_ch: 那是啊，长得好看真的了不起，您真好看！
t_en: Exactly! Being good-looking sometimes does give you privileges. You've got it!

t_ch: …你们该不是画饼骗我吧？
t_en: ...Are you sure you're not just flattering me?

t_ch: 呃，抱歉，我也只是听来的，现在赛事要紧，这些方案可能八字还没一撇呢，只是听他们在讲。不过我觉得您这种身份，来打广告实在是屈尊了……
t_en: Uh, sorry, I just heard it through the grapevine. This whole endorsement thing is still in its early stages. Back to business, this event at hand is more important right now. But I gotta say, someone like you doing ads would be a real waste...

t_ch: 啊，时间也差不多，总决赛要开赛了，组委会那边在催了。
t_en: Ah, looks like it's almost time. The finals are about to start and the committee's been rushing me.

t_ch: 抱歉我有点太激动了，一想到您要拿冠军就有点兴奋，希望没耽误您做赛前准备。
t_en: Sorry, I got a bit too excited. Just thinking about you winning the championship gets me pumped! Hope I didn't mess up your pre-race prep.

t_ch: 您准备好了请告诉我——
t_en: Let me know when you're ready—

t_ch: {NICKNAME}贵宾，等一下！我有件紧急事项要向您汇报。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, please hold on. I have an urgent matter for you.

t_ch: 我确认了您那位认识的黑客，刚刚骇入赛事系统，调整了分组名单，您和他将在总决赛正面对上。
t_en: I've confirmed the hacker you know just hacked into the event system and adjusted the groupings. You and your friend will face off directly in the finals.

t_ch: 此人赛车叫「专业代打」，但赛前检查时，我们发现球笼赛车里没有驾驶员。
t_en: This person's racing car is called "Professional Substitute," but during the pre-race inspection, we discovered that there was no driver in the Spheroid racing car.

t_ch: 她是黑客，那是远程操控系统…
t_en: She is a hacker, and that is a remote control system...

t_ch: 别担心，只是普通的闹鬼罢了！
t_en: Don't worry, it's just an ordinary case of haunting!

t_ch: 我们想取消它的参赛资格，但黑客把系统锁定了，如果您觉得有安全隐患……
t_en: We wanted to disqualify it from the race, but the hacker locked the system. If you feel there are safety concerns...

t_ch: 让她启动引擎，陪我跑一圈吧。
t_en: Let her start the engine and take a lap with me.

t_ch: 您这么有信心，那我就放心了。
t_en: Since you're so confident, I feel reassured.

t_ch: 我太懂她了…不急，跟她耍耍。
t_en: I know her too well... No rush, let's play along with her.

t_ch: 既然您这么说，那我就放心了。
t_en: Since you said so, I feel reassured.

t_ch: 那么比赛继续，祝您赛场上一路顺风。
t_en: Then the race will continue. Wishing you smooth sailing on the track.

t_ch: 「专业代打」号并没有停下而是向远方驶去——像是接受她的邀请，你紧随其后。
t_en: The "Professional Substitute" didn't stop but instead drove off into the distance — as if accepting her invitation, you followed closely behind.

t_ch: 差不多就在这里吧，这也没有别人。我本来打算和你见一面的，不过现在这样也挺好。
t_en: Let's stop here. There's no one else around anyway. I originally planned to meet you in person, but this way works just fine too.

t_ch: 从一开始就知道是你在搞鬼了。
t_en: I knew it was you causing trouble from the very beginning.

t_ch: 别这么说嘛，你都拿到该拿的任务奖励了，别这么挑剔嘛。
t_en: Don't say that. You've already received the mission rewards you were supposed to get. Don't be so picky.

t_ch: 所以，就是你给我报名参赛的？
t_en: So, you're the one who signed me up for the competition?

t_ch: 其实是个意外，说来话长。
t_en: It was actually an accident. It's a long story.

t_ch: 我最近组装了套超棒的赛车游戏外设，把《球笼飞车》和《模拟球笼竞速：<unbreak>26</unbreak>》的模块都整合进去了。
t_en: I recently assembled an awesome set of racing game peripherals, integrating modules from "Spheroid Racing" and "Simulated Spheroid Racing: <unbreak>26</unbreak>."

t_ch: 我本想着远程接入真正的球笼赛车比赛，试试实车手感…再说了，你们星穹列车那帮人不是还在闲逛吗？我就借个假身份自己玩玩，没想到你自己过来了。
t_en: I originally planned to remotely access a real Spheroid racing competition to test the feel of an actual car... Besides, aren't you guys from the Astral Express still wandering around? I just borrowed a fake identity to have some fun myself, but I didn't expect you to show up in person.

t_ch: 我记得你游戏技术不错，能碰上我这么强的对手，玩得开心吗？
t_en: I remember your gaming skills are pretty good. Having encountered such a strong opponent like me, did you have fun?

t_ch: 挺开心的，作为对手还算不错。
t_en: I had a great time, you're quite a decent opponent.

t_ch: 哈！我就知道你的游戏品位不会太差。
t_en: Ha! I knew your taste in games couldn't be that bad.

t_ch: 别转移话题，你不许借我的号！
t_en: Don't change the subject, you're not allowed to borrow my account!

t_ch: 哎呀，我本来想的是，拿了冠军就把冠军奖励送给你的。
t_en: Oops, I originally thought that if I won the championship, I'd give the prize to you.

t_ch: 既然你能自己拿下冠军奖励，我干脆就把这定制的球笼赛车送给你作为赔礼吧。
t_en: Since you managed to win the championship reward on your own, I'll just give you this custom Spheroid racing car as an apology.

t_ch: 一开始呢，我是想来帮你代打的，但是吧，报名的时候我发现赛车模块可以完全DIY。
t_en: At first, I wanted to help you as a substitute, but when I signed up, I realized the racing module could be completely DIY.

t_ch: 一折腾起改装，人就会上瘾，钱也花了不少…所以——我向你保证这辆球笼赛车是你见过的最好的，你拿去好好开吧。
t_en: Once you start modifying, you'll get hooked, and end up spending quite a bit of money... so I promise you this Spheroid race car is the best you've ever seen, so go ahead and drive it well.

t_ch: …这辆赛车里还搭载了普罗米修斯同款远程操纵模块，带有联机功能——我设置成局域网了，只有你和我能联机。
t_en: ...This race car is also equipped with the same remote control module as Prometheus, featuring an online connection function. I've set it to a local network, so only you and I can connect.

t_ch: 车钥匙拿好，别丢了。
t_en: Take the car key, don't lose it.

t_ch: 你就不怕被公司发现？
t_en: Aren't you afraid the IPC will find out?

t_ch: 那我就这么开回去？
t_en: So I just drive it back like this?

t_ch: 怕什么？他们连我在哪都找不到，就他们那个层层外包的效率，想找到我，下辈子吧！
t_en: What is there to be afraid of? They can't even locate me. With their layers of outsourcing and inefficiency, they might find me... in their next life!

t_ch: 车你直接开回去就好了，但别说是我送的。我倒是无所谓，但要是让人知道你和我走得太近…总归对你有负面影响。
t_en: Just drive the car back, but don't say it was from me. I don't really care, but if people find out you're that close with me... it'll only have negative consequences for you.

t_ch: 没关系，我不介意。
t_en: It's okay, I don't mind.

t_ch: 行，你自己注意。
t_en: Alright, just take care of yourself.

t_ch: …你注意安全。
t_en: ...You stay safe.

t_ch: 放心啦，我玩够了就该下了。
t_en: Don't worry, once I've had my fun, I'll log off.

t_ch: 刚刚跟你一起玩还挺开心的，你这家伙车技真不错，有空一起开车？呃，我是说赛车游戏。
t_en: I had a lot of fun playing with you just now. Your driving skills are really impressive. Want to drive together again sometime? Uh, I mean in a racing game.

t_ch: 不过现在我得走了，要是让某些烦人的家伙追踪到信号可就不好玩了。回头有空我们再联机吧。
t_en: But I have to go now. It wouldn't be fun if some annoying people managed to track the signal. Let's play online again when we have time.

t_ch: 下了，拜拜！
t_en: Logging off, bye!

t_ch: 您没事吧！我看到您的赛车跟着那辆「专业代打」开出去，就、就赶过来了。
t_en: Are you alright? I saw your race car follow that Professional Substitute out, so I... I rushed over!

t_ch: 咦？这是…那辆球笼赛车？什么情况？
t_en: Huh? This is... that Spheroid race car? What's going on?

t_ch: （隐瞒这段车的来历）
t_en: (Conceal the origin of this car.)

t_ch: 我不知道这个车怎么来的，你说，它是怎么来的……
t_en: I don't know where this car came from. How did you think it got here?

t_ch: 这是那个黑客送我的车…
t_en: This is the car that hacker gave me...

t_ch: 通缉犯送您…的车吗？这个事情倒也不必声张，我想想……
t_en: A wanted criminal gave you... this car? Well, there's no need to make a big deal out of it. Let me think...

t_ch: 您看这样如何——对方弃赛，您见猎心喜，我们为您登记入库，作为冠军奖励。
t_en: How about this, your opponent forfeited the race, you seized the opportunity, and we'll register the car as part of the official inventory, labeling it as a champion's reward?

t_ch: 这样一来，您「没看到」，也「没见过」，公司就不会来找您，省得给您添麻烦。
t_en: This way, you "haven't seen" it and "never encountered" it, so the IPC won't come looking for you, saving you from unnecessary trouble.

t_ch: 就按你说的办吧。
t_en: Let's do it your way.

t_ch: 你们还挺上道的。
t_en: You're pretty sharp about these things.

t_ch: 入库手续正在为您加急办理了，差点忘了正事——恭喜您登上冠军宝座！
t_en: Your registration's being fast-tracked. Almost forgot to mention the important stuff — congrats on making it to the championship!

t_ch: 「极速杯」冠军诞生后，咱们还有娱乐性质的挑战赛。您的成绩已记录在排行榜上，其他选手会以此为目标发起挑战，您也可再次参赛，刷新纪录！
t_en: After the "Speed Cup" champion is crowned, we also have some entertainment, focusing on challenge races. Your record has been logged on the leaderboard, and other racers will aim to challenge it. You can also compete again to set a new record!

t_ch: 当然，您参不参加挑战赛，全凭您自己决定，请您不要有任何心理负担。
t_en: Of course, whether you participate in the challenge races is entirely up to you. Please don't feel any pressure about it.

t_ch: 我谨代表家族再次祝贺您获得冠军！您的冠军奖励也已准备就绪，请您及时领取！
t_en: On behalf of The Family, I once again congratulate you on winning the championship! Your champion rewards are also ready, please make sure to claim them in time!

t_ch: 对了，电视台那边有个冠军专访，劳驾您跟大家说两句——请随我来，电视台的采访间就在会场旁边。
t_en: Oh right. There's an exclusive championship interview over at the TV station. Please say a thing or two to the audience — come with me. The interview room for the station is right next to the arena.

t_ch: 麻烦看下镜头！
t_en: Look at the camera, please!

t_ch: 可以给我签个名吗？
t_en: Can I please have your autograph?

t_ch: 来了来了！我们的新科冠军终于登场了！观众朋友们，让我们欢迎「极速杯」冠军—— {NICKNAME}！
t_en: They're here! Our new champion is finally here! My dear audience, let us welcome the champion of the Speed Cup — {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 您的比赛表现真的太精彩了，解说之前都快把嗓子喊哑了！大家都对您的驾驶技巧赞不绝口！
t_en: Your performance was simply marvelous. The commentator almost blew their voice out with how hard they were cheering! Everyone was praising your driving skills!

t_ch: 来，麻烦您看下镜头——恭喜您夺得冠军！此刻的心情一定非常激动吧？您有什么话想对现场的观众和屏幕前的粉丝们说吗？
t_en: Here, please look at the camera — congratulations on winning the championship! I imagine you must be so excited right now! Do you have anything to say to the audience at the venue and at home?

t_ch: 我的一小步，无名客的一大步！
t_en: One small step for me, a giant leap for the Nameless!

t_ch: 哇哦！这番话可太有气势了，颇有一种开创时代的感觉！这就是无名客所践行的开拓精神吧！
t_en: Woah! That line sounds so epic! It's like you're defining a whole era! Is this the trailblazing spirit that the Nameless lives by?

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 咳，一般来说，不爱说话是高手的常见特征之一。看来咱们的冠军更倾向于用实力说话，不喜欢多言！好，那我们就用掌声再次祝贺{NICKNAME}！
t_en: *cough* Well, powerful figures often prefer to save their words. Looks like our champion would rather speak their actions! Alright then. Let's just put our hands together for {NICKNAME} once more!

t_ch: 呃，大家比赛快乐？
t_en: Um. Happy championship?

t_ch: 哈哈！我们的冠军年纪还小，也有点激动，不过我想大家都能听懂——大家听到了吧？记得在赛道上享受比赛的乐趣，赢得开心，挑战也要快乐！
t_en: Ahahaha! Our champion is young, and a bit too excited, but I think you can all get what {F#s}he meant — Did we hear that? Remember the joy you feel on the racetrack and win happiness for yourselves! You need to enjoy the challenge!

t_ch: 好的，非常感谢{NICKNAME}接受我们的采访！接下来就让我们的冠军好好休息吧！
t_en: Alright. Thank you {NICKNAME} for coming to this interview! Now we'll let you rest up, dear champion!

t_ch: 但是…「极速杯」的赛道永远不会停歇，新的挑战已经在前方等着所有赛车手们！
t_en: However, the racetrack for the Speed Cup will never end. There are already new challenges waiting for all the racers up ahead!

t_ch: 今天，我们见证了一位新的冠军诞生，但未来谁能打破纪录，超越巅峰？让我们拭目以待！
t_en: We have witnessed the birth of a new champion today, but who will break their record and claim the throne in the future? We look forward to that with bated breath.

t_ch: 您好，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}！我是星际和平公司派来与您交接的工作人员莱娜——您可能不认识我，毕竟此前我都是和「{TEXTJOIN#87}」大副沟通的。
t_en: Hey there, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}! I'm Lena from the Interastral Peace Corporation, here to touch base with you — We haven't actually met before since I've only been communicating with the first mate of the {TEXTJOIN#87} up till now.

t_ch: …不知道您是否还记得名为「折纸小鸟对对碰」的集换式街机对战游戏？之前鸢尾花家系的缇娜负责的那个「啾啾，啾啾……」。
t_en: ...Do you happen to remember that arcade game "Origami Bird Clash" with the collectible items? The one Tinah from the Iris Family was running, with all the "Tweet, tweet..."

t_ch: 我当然还记得，三消那个对吧？
t_en: Of course I do. The Match-3 game, right?

t_ch: 您记得就好，看来省去了不少解释的功夫。
t_en: Perfect. That saves me a lengthy explanation.

t_ch: 不好意思，我有些记不太清楚。
t_en: Sorry, it's not ringing any bells.

t_ch: 没关系。您贵人多忘事，「折纸小鸟对对碰」是很受欢迎的三消对战街机游戏。
t_en: That's perfectly fine. Someone in your position has countless things to keep track of. "Origami Bird Clash" is a popular Match-3 arcade game.

t_ch: 折纸鸟…小啾啾……
t_en: Origami bird... Hey, little birdie...

t_ch: …不知道您在说些什么呢。
t_en: ...I'm afraid I have absolutely no idea what you're talking about.

t_ch: 难道不是你先说的吗？
t_en: Weren't you the one who mentioned these birds?

t_ch: 为了节约您的时间，我就长话短说了——公司已经投资了「折纸小鸟对对碰」，并希望在全银河中推广这款匹诺康尼风情的街机游戏！
t_en: Let me cut to the chase for the sake of time. The IPC has invested in "Origami Bird Clash" and is planning to launch this Penacony-inspired arcade sensation across the cosmos!

t_ch: 眼下正值游戏上线之际，作为匹诺康尼股东，也作为测试版合作方，公司将在您的「{TEXTJOIN#87}」上开设所谓街机厅的「元祖店」！当然，它的所属权都是在您的名下！
t_en: With the game launching soon, the IPC will set up the original arcade store on your property, {TEXTJOIN#87}, since you're both a Penacony shareholder and beta testing partner! And don't worry — it'll be completely under your ownership!

t_ch: 一切经营费用，一切经营管理，都不需要您亲自操心…您只需要在这里每天打游戏，躺着挣钱就可以了！
t_en: No need to worry about any expenses or management headaches... Just show up, play some games, and collect your earnings while chilling out!

t_ch: 咱们事不宜迟，现在就立刻体验一下这次的新内容吧！
t_en: Let's not waste another second — time to try out the new content!

t_ch: 来来来，我已经等不及了！
t_en: Let's go. I'm pumped!

t_ch: 为了恭贺新店开业，慈玉女士也授权了「翡翠小鸟」赠予咱们这家「元祖店」！
t_en: To celebrate our grand opening, Lady Bonajade has graciously granted our original store the right to use the Jadebird!

t_ch: 凡是来「{TEXTJOIN#87}」的游客，都将有机会免费体验它。真是太棒了，不是吗？
t_en: Anyone who visits {TEXTJOIN#87} gets to experience it for free! Amazing, right?

t_ch: 事不宜迟，咱们这就把「街机厅」开起来吧！别让我们的客人等太久了！
t_en: Let's get this arcade up and running ASAP! Can't keep the customers waiting!

t_ch: 感觉你们好像一直很着急！
t_en: Why are you in such a rush?

t_ch: 哎呀，老板您有所不知，现在街机厅的玩家就喜欢快速上手的活动！
t_en: C'mon, boss. Arcade players these days like games that they can pick up and play!

t_ch: 合着这半天，还没开店呢？
t_en: So after all this talk, we haven't even opened yet?

t_ch: 咱们还等着您到店剪彩呢，那现在算是天赐良机…咱们立刻开店。
t_en: We've been waiting for you to do the ribbon-cutting ceremony! Now that you're here — perfect timing. Let's open immediately.

t_ch: 您就在一旁玩着游戏等待，咱们的筑梦师工程小队立刻展开施工——
t_en: Just enjoy some games while you wait. Our Dreamweaver engineering team is starting construction as we speak —

t_ch: 「折纸小鸟对对碰•元祖店」的施工十分顺利，你刚进匹配界面，便已完工了。
t_en: The "Origami Bird Clash: Original Shop" construction finished in record time — by the time you entered the matchmaking screen, everything was already done.

t_ch: 可算赶上了…真是千钧一发啊！多亏我福大命大…当然，也少不了你的功劳。
t_en: Phew, made it by a hair! Talk about cutting it close... Thank goodness for my incredible luck... and you helped too, of course.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我的{F#好姐妹}{M#好兄弟}，救救，救救！这匹诺康尼有邪物作祟啊……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, my {F#bestie}{M#bro}, HELP ME! Something seriously evil is happening in Penacony!

t_ch: 「四尺圣堂」复活，找上你了？
t_en: Did the "Four-Foot Hall" come back to haunt you?

t_ch: 不是。你可别提那次痛苦回忆了，这次是新的痛苦回忆。
t_en: No way. Let's not dig up that nightmare. This is a brand-new nightmare, thank you very much.

t_ch: 说是救命，不就只是在打游戏？
t_en: You call this "needing help," but you're just playing a video game.

t_ch: 我知道…在世俗的眼光里，可能看不出我有性命危机，只觉得我玩得上瘾。
t_en: I know... To normal people, I probably don't look like I'm in mortal danger — just addicted to this game.

t_ch: 你先解释解释什么叫「邪物」？
t_en: What exactly is this "evil" you're talking about?

t_ch: …各位请看，给你们介绍一下我在匹诺康尼认识的「新、朋、友」——幽灵小鸟。
t_en: Folks, meet my "new best friend," whom I got to know in Penacony — the Ghost Bird.

t_ch: 啾啾！
t_en: Tweet tweet!

t_ch: 我被这只幽灵小鸟给诅咒了！呜呜呜……
t_en: I'm about to be CURSED by this Ghost Bird! Waaah...

t_ch: 匹诺康尼在家族管理下，梦境可是绝对安全的，青雀小姐，你说的这诅咒是…？
t_en: The Family ensures Penacony's Dreamscape is <unbreak>100%</unbreak> safe, Miss Qingque. What exactly is this curse you're talking about?

t_ch: 名为<color=#dbc291ff>「不打游戏我就要死了」</color>的诅咒！
t_en: The dreaded <color=#dbc291ff>"No-Game-No-Life"</color> curse!

t_ch: 啥叫「不打游戏我就要死了」？
t_en: No-Game-No-Life? What's that supposed to mean?

t_ch: 就是字面意义上的——如果我没有继续打游戏，我就会在匹诺康尼立刻暴毙。
t_en: Literally what it sounds like — stop playing and I die on the spot in Penacony. Simple as that.

t_ch: 总之，如你们所见，这只幽灵小鸟强行与我建立了朋友关系……
t_en: Long story short, this Ghost Bird decided we're friends now, whether I like it or not...

t_ch: 换言之就是「施加了诅咒」，而这只小鸟就是那个…咒主。
t_en: Basically, I'm "cursed," and this feathery little menace is the one who... cast it.

t_ch: 啾啾！
t_en: Tweet tweet!

t_ch: 虽然你们只能听到可爱的两声「啾啾」，但它实际上在说「不和我继续玩下去的话，就去死吧！我会像抽油烟机一样把你的灵魂全部抽走……」
t_en: You hear innocent "tweet tweets" now, but what it's really saying is: "Play with me or DIE! I'll suck your soul out like a kitchen exhaust fan on full blast..."

t_ch: …这句「啾啾」的信息量也太大了。
t_en: Wow... that's a lot of information packed into just "tweet tweet."

t_ch: 放心，匹诺康尼是绝对安全的！
t_en: Relax! Penacony is totally, absolutely safe!

t_ch: 如果我没有被「四尺圣堂」绑架过，我就真的信了这句话了。
t_en: I'd buy that if I hadn't been kidnapped by the "Four-Foot Hall" that time, thank you very much.

t_ch: 其实真正的问题是——即便在现实酒店醒过来，幽灵小鸟的诅咒依然存在……
t_en: Here's the real problem — even waking up in the hotel in reality won't break the Ghost Bird's curse...

t_ch: 啾啾…啾啾啾！
t_en: Tweet tweet... tweet tweet tweet!

t_ch: 虽然你们只能听到可爱的两声「啾啾」，但它实际上在说「你休想摆脱我的掌控，你还是抓紧时间打游戏吧，这样才能活下去……」
t_en: You hear innocent "tweet tweets" now, but it's saying: "You'll NEVER escape me, so keep playing if you value your life..."

t_ch: …看来只能照着小鸟说的做了呢。
t_en: I guess we'd better do exactly what the bird wants.

t_ch: 我才刚开业，诅咒太不吉利了…
t_en: Great, we just had the grand opening and now there's an unlucky curse...

t_ch: 这银河里哪里有「吉利的诅咒」啊喂！吉利的那叫祝福！
t_en: As if there's such a thing as a "lucky curse" anywhere in the cosmos! If it's lucky, it's called a blessing, genius!

t_ch: 我被幽灵小鸟诅咒了，你被我给诅咒了！{F#好姐妹}{M#好兄弟}，要死一起死……
t_en: The Ghost Bird cursed me, and now I've cursed you! {F#Bestie}{M#Bro}, we're gonna go down together...

t_ch: 啾啾啾！
t_en: Tweet! Tweet!

t_ch: 谢谢你，幽灵小鸟。但是「我俩是同行」这句话，请不要用在你的诅咒对象上。
t_en: Appreciate that, Ghost Bird. But maybe don't say "See? We're in the same profession" when talking about people you've cursed.

t_ch: 你别担心，毕竟这里是街机厅！
t_en: Chill out! That's what arcades are for!

t_ch: 那青雀你要怎么解除诅咒呢，需要我联系太卜大人吗？
t_en: Any ideas how to break this curse, Qingque? Want me to call the Master Diviner?

t_ch: 别别别，别告诉她老人家，她还以为我在曜青仙舟度假呢！
t_en: Absolutely not! She thinks I'm having a nice relaxing time on the Xianzhou Yaoqing!

t_ch: 我在匹诺康尼也认识了一些朋友，我已经给他们发消息了，他们会来救我的！
t_en: I know people in Penacony now, and I've already texted them for help. My cavalry's on the way!

t_ch: …青雀小姐来得可真是时候，这里刚布置成面向所有宾客的「街机厅」，眼下倒是刚好可以解决您的燃眉之急。
t_en: ...Talk about perfect timing, Miss Qingque. We just set up this "arcade" for everyone to enjoy, which solves your emergency perfectly.

t_ch: 好好好，我就知道{F#好姐妹}{M#好兄弟}{NICKNAME}不会放着我不管的——
t_en: Perfect! I knew my {F#girl}{M#guy} {NICKNAME} wouldn't leave me hanging—

t_ch: 不好意思打断一下，但青雀小姐说的该不会就是这两位吧？
t_en: Sorry to cut in, but are these two individuals the "cavalry" you were referring to, Miss Qingque?

t_ch: 宝了个贝的，那就赶紧开始吧，还废话什么呢？
t_en: Holy forkin' shirtballs, can we just begin already? What's with all the yapping?

t_ch: 挚友，青雀女士，好久不见。眼下不是打招呼的时候，我们直接开始「救援」吧。
t_en: Miss Qingque. Friend. Good to see you both. No time for small talk — let's start working on the "rescue" right away.

t_ch: 宝了个贝的，所以光是打这破游戏没有办法解除诅咒是吗？
t_en: Fudge me sideways, so we can't actually solve the curse by playing this game?

t_ch: 注意你的言辞，波提欧兄弟。根据我的观察，咱们和青雀女士的对局，只是在给她续命。
t_en: Mind your language, Boothill. As far as I can tell, our games with Miss Qingque are just keeping her alive, not solving the problem.

t_ch: 不好意思，给两位添麻烦了……
t_en: I'm so sorry to drag you all into this...

t_ch: 抱歉，青雀女士。我与波提欧兄弟并非破除邪祟的「专家」，帮不了你多少。
t_en: My apologies, Miss Qingque. Neither Boothill nor I specialize in exorcism, so there's only so much we can do.

t_ch: …不过感谢纯美女神伊德莉拉，我们也结识了不少侠义之士正在赶来的路上，他们之中肯定有人能帮助到你！
t_en: But thankfully, praise Idrila the Beauty, we've made friends with plenty of skilled heroes who are headed here now. One of them must know how to help!

t_ch: 少呜呜伯的废话了，仙舟小妹，你到底怎么招惹上诅咒的？
t_en: Enough screwbbaboo! Spill it, Xianzhou kiddo — how'd you get yourself cursed?

t_ch: …你们知道「匹诺康尼百大不可思议」吗？那其中就有「折纸小鸟」的都市传说。
t_en: Ever heard of the "Penacony Top <unbreak>100</unbreak> Mysteries"? There's an "Origami Bird" urban legend on that list.

t_ch: 要破除诅咒的话，信息不够。
t_en: We'll need more details than that if we're going to break this curse.

t_ch: 没错，我们需要对诅咒的触发条件、诅咒诞生的根源等等信息调查一番。
t_en: Right. We need to understand what triggered the curse, where it came from, and gather more details.

t_ch: 当地的不可思议有点太多了…
t_en: This Penacony place has way too many mysteries...

t_ch: 你可别嫌多，这「百大不可思议」每年还会更换呢。
t_en: You think that's a lot? They refresh the "Top <unbreak>100</unbreak> Mysteries" list every single year.

t_ch: 行了行了，他宝贝的，搞那么麻烦做什么——喂，那什么小鸟，你能听懂我说话吧？
t_en: Enough, enough, you muddle-fudger, stop making this so complex! Hey, bird, you understand what I'm saying, don't you?

t_ch: 啾啾啾？
t_en: Tweet tweet?

t_en: You like games, right? Let's have another match. If you win, you can have my soul. But if I win, get the fudge outta here and leave us alone.

t_ch: 等等，波提欧兄弟，破除诅咒没有这么简单……
t_en: Wait, Boothill, breaking a curse isn't that simple...

t_ch: 少他宝贝的废话，小可爱，就说你敢不敢吧？
t_en: Cut the shirtball, you son of a bench! Yes or no?

t_ch: 啾！
t_en: Tweet!

t_ch: 它说「可以再玩一局」。
t_en: It's saying "I can't turn down a game!"

t_ch: 让他宝贝的试试吧。
t_en: Yeah, the horsefeathers can try!

t_ch: 哈哈，就这？瞧你那嚣张样，还以为我得多爱爱你呢——回去好好练练吧，小可爱！
t_en: Ha! That all you got? With all that swagger, I thought I'd have to butter your fudge, you shirt-for-brains! Go back to bird school, cutie!

t_ch: 啾啾？
t_en: Tweet tweet?

t_ch: 它说「我没有说过，你赢了就可以解除诅咒」。那个，它确实没有说过……
t_en: It's saying "I never agreed that winning would lift the curse." And to be fair, it never actually promised that...

t_ch: 幽灵小鸟阁下，这就是你的不对了。波提欧兄弟已经通过公平的对决赢下了胜利…愿赌服输，能够坦率接受自己的失败，也是一种「纯美」。
t_en: Now that's not fair, Ghost Bird. Boothill beat you fair and square... being a good sport about losing is part of "the Beauty," you know.
...Tweet?

t_ch: 玩你呜呜伯的文字游戏呢。看清楚了！究竟是谁拿着枪，究竟是谁掌握了话语权。
t_en: Playing word games, fudging-serious-ly? You see this? Who's holding the gun, huh? Who do you think makes the rules here?

t_ch: 宝贝了个腿的，你要是讲义气，咱们还能交个朋友。现在，赶紧哪儿来的回哪儿去……
t_en: Look, you horsefeathers, we could've been buddies if you weren't a cheater. Now get lost, back to wherever you came from...

t_ch: …波提欧兄弟？
t_en: Boothill?

t_ch: 这…不是吧……
t_en: No... Boothill!

t_ch: 他宝贝的，还挺可爱呀。
t_en: Son of a fudge, that's actually pretty cute.

t_ch: 当真不愧是「纯美」眷顾之人——在褪去那副冰冷的钢铁躯壳后，你的身姿依旧是如此令人过目不忘……
t_en: As expected of someone blessed by the Beauty — even without your cold, metal body, you're still quite the eye-catcher...

t_ch: 啾？*愤怒的鸟语*——啾！
t_en: Tweet? *Angry bird speak* — Tweet!

t_ch: …坏了坏了，这下坏了。
t_en: Well, that's not good. Not good at all.

t_ch: 啾？*愤怒的鸟语*——啾！
t_en: Tweet? *Angry bird speak* — Tweet!

t_ch: 稍安勿躁，波提欧兄弟。至少现在你愤怒的鸟鸣，比之前口不择言时要更加动听。
t_en: Settle down, Boothill. I must say, your angry tweeting is more musical than your prior colorful vocabulary.

t_ch: 这可真是抱歉了…本来只是想请你们帮忙的，没想到还让你们也陷入危机了。
t_en: I feel terrible... I only wanted your help, and now you're caught up in this nightmare too.

t_ch: 别担心，即便波提欧兄弟化作折纸小鸟，他那火热而正直的心也愿意助我们一臂之力！
t_en: No worries! Even as an Origami Bird, Boothill's hot-blooded, justice-loving heart is still on our side!

t_ch: *鸟语花香*！
t_en: *Some truly fowl words*

t_ch: 青雀女士你就先继续陪幽灵小鸟玩下去吧，我们找来的帮手就快到…嗯？
t_en: Miss Qingque! Just keep the Ghost Bird busy with games until then! Help is on the way... huh?

t_ch: 缭乱忍侠•AKA乱破，参上！
t_en: Dazzling Ninja a.k.a. Rappa, reporting for duty!

t_ch: 多么纯粹的邪祟啊！没想到，诚如密信所言，此处遭遇了恶灵式神•妖鸟。
t_en: What a being of absolute evil! Exactly as the secret letter warned, this place is plagued by the Evil Spirit Demon Bird!

t_ch: 说起来，球棒•忍者只是新店开业便遇上此等厄运，阁下不愧是灾厄体质。
t_en: So, Baseball Bat Ninja, your shop has just opened and is immediately facing trouble. You really do attract bad luck, don't you?

t_ch: 其实…遇上这等厄运的是我，啊…还有波提欧先生。
t_en: Actually... I'm the curse magnet here, and... poor Mr. Boothill too.
$%Tweet, R^$%!

t_ch: 这…当真是银枪•修罗殿下？！在下推测，这定是某种类似邪猿变身的忍术。
t_en: Wait... THIS is Silvergun Shura!? Surely this is some ninjutsu like the ape transformation.

t_ch: …如果这真是模因污染，让我来尝试为游侠调律吧。
t_en: ...If this is truly a memetic corruption, perhaps I can try tuning the Ranger.

t_ch: …不行，眼下如果强行干涉，只会造成不可逆转的伤害。现在该如何是好？
t_en: ...No. Forcing anything now could cause permanent damage. What options do we have?

t_ch: 折纸鸟专家！我需要专家。
t_en: I need an Origami Bird expert! Stat!

t_ch: 我在折大里进修过匹诺康尼史，关于折纸鸟，我记得一些……
t_en: I took Penaconian history courses at Paperfold University, so I know some things about Origami Birds...

t_en: In the Clockie series, the five Origami Birds studied under Professor Owl in Dreamville.

t_ch: 幽灵小鸟根本就不在匹诺康尼的历史体系里，换句话说，它肯定是只外来的鸟。
t_en: Ghost Bird doesn't exist in any Penacony historical records, which means it's definitely from the outside.

t_ch: 谢谢你，潮流•忍徒。
t_en: Thanks for the insight, Trendy Ninja.

t_ch: 连家族的调律也没能生效吗？
t_en: So even The Family's tuning didn't work?

t_ch: …这只「幽灵小鸟」的诅咒有些特殊，已经对现实中游侠的躯体产生了影响。
t_en: This Ghost Bird's curse is different. It's already affecting the Ranger's real body outside the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 我去找其他善于调律的家伙问问情况，可能需要去现实酒店里调查一下……
t_en: I need to talk to other tuning experts about this. We probably need to investigate the hotel in reality too...

t_ch: 谢谢你，酌人•忍者。
t_en: I appreciate your help, Sommelier Ninja.

t_ch: 乱破…你应该不会变成小鸟。
t_en: Rappa... You're probably not going to become a bird.

t_ch: 原来如此！看来{NICKNAME}•忍者已经识破了恶灵式神•妖鸟的弱点。
t_en: Brilliant! Ninja {NICKNAME} has already figured out the Evil Spirit Demon Bird's weakness!

t_ch: 在下跨越银河迢迢特地赶来此地，定不辜负使命。接下来就交给在下吧。
t_en: I have traveled across the farthest reaches of the cosmos to arrive here, and I will not fail in my mission. Now, leave the rest to me.

t_ch: 恶灵式神•折纸鸟阁下！你是否敢于接受在下的挑战？
t_en: Evil Spirit Origami Bird! Do you have the courage to accept my challenge?

t_ch: 啾！
t_en: Tweet!

t_en: Very well! Since you have accepted my challenge, and to avoid repeating Lord Silvergun Shura's tragedy, I must lay down some rules before we begin!

t_ch: 啾啾啾！
t_en: Tweet! Tweet!

t_ch: 此次破除咒法之胜负，必须遵循公平公义之忍道，不义之人叛誓必将吞下千枚银针！
t_en: For this contest to break the curse, we shall uphold the just and honorable way of the ninja. Should one betray the vow, a thousand silver needles shall be swallowed in penance!

t_ch: …竟然你一句我一句地聊起来了！你一定要加油啊，银河忍者小姐姐——
t_en: ...Wait, they're actually having a conversation!? You've got this, Miss Galactic Ninja —

t_ch: 就靠你击败它了，乱破。
t_en: We're counting on you to take it down, Rappa!

t_ch: 是在下赢了，恶灵式神•妖鸟！
t_en: I have emerged victorious, Evil Spirit Demon Bird!

t_ch: 嘎嘎嘎嘎嘎！
t_en: Bwahahaha!

t_ch: 这句我听明白了，应该是波提欧兄弟豪迈的笑声。
t_en: I understood that — it must be Boothill's hearty laugh.
Tweet...

t_en: It said, "I've lost. A bet's a bet."

t_ch: 那么恶灵式神•妖鸟阁下请遵守与在下之约定，尽公平公义之忍道！
t_en: Then, Evil Spirit Demon Bird, I ask you to honor our pact — uphold the just and honorable way of the ninja!

t_ch: 啾啾啾。
t_en: Tweet tweet tweet.

t_ch: 它说「如果我战胜了你，我将抽取你的灵魂，将你变成折纸小鸟。」
t_en: It said, "Had I defeated you, I would have claimed your soul and turned you into an Origami Bird."

t_ch: 啾啾。
t_en: Tweet tweet.

t_ch: 「既然你战胜了我，为了公平起见，我也应该付出相同的代价！」
t_en: "Now that you've bested me, in fairness, I too must pay the same price!"

t_ch: 「我决定，让我的好朋友——青雀，变成折纸小鸟……」
t_en: "I've made up my mind... I'm turning my dear friend Qingque into an Origami Bird..."

t_ch: 啾啾啾……
t_en: Tweet tweet tweet...

t_ch: 等等！什么叫「对不起，我没能保护好你」啊喂！你不会真的要……
t_en: Hold up! What do you mean by "I'm sorry I couldn't protect you"!? Don't tell me you're actually gonna...

t_ch: 麻雀•忍者——！
t_en: Sparrow Ninja!

t_ch: 啾啾！啾啾！
t_en: Tweet tweet! Tweet tweet!

t_ch: 我听懂了，这句是「救救」！
t_en: I got that one, it means "Save me!"

t_ch: 是「救救！救救！」
t_en: It's "Save me! Save me!"

t_ch: …名字最符合人设的一集。
t_en: ...Her name really stays true to her character.

t_ch: 噗。
t_en: Pfft.

t_ch: 银枪•修罗殿下……
t_en: Lord Silvergun Shura...

t_ch: 那现在我们还救不救啊？
t_en: So, are we still saving her or not?

t_ch: 啾！啾！
t_en: Tweet! Tweet!

t_ch: 她说「还是要救啊」！
t_en: She said, "Of course you've got to save me!"

t_ch: 诸位莫要惊慌，我联系的「怪异专家」已经来了，你们看，这几位是谁——！
t_en: Everyone, stay calm! The "anomaly experts" I called are here. Look closely and see who they are —!

t_ch: 啾啾啾……
t_en: Tweet tweet tweet...

t_ch: 呵呵，原来邪祟就是你呀。
t_en: Well, well... so you were the fiendling all along.

t_ch: 青雀小姐…小鸟，破除诅咒还需要些时间，请你一定要继续坚持玩下去，坚持就是胜利！
t_en: Miss Qingque... Qingqird, it will take a bit more time to dispel this curse. Please continue to play and persevere. Victory comes to those who persist!

t_ch: 啾啾！
t_en: Tweet tweet!

t_en: Boss, whaddaya think?

t_en: It has an arcade vibe now...

t_en: That's right. We even kept the white-box tester. It's practically an Origami Bird Clash History Museum.

t_ch: 你们这游戏也没有那么长的历史。
t_en: Your game isn't even that old.

t_en: Pretty bare-bones for an original store!

t_en: We thought it captured a bit of the old "{TEXTJOIN#87}" flavor. Only then can folks realize they are in the {TEXTJOIN#87}.

t_ch: 那么，我们准备开业了……
t_en: Alright. We're going to open for business...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#好姐妹}{M#好兄弟}！救救，救救——！
t_en: {F#Sister}{M#Brother} {NICKNAME}! Save me, save meeee!

t_ch: 青雀别慌，我来助你。
t_en: Don't worry, Qingque. I've got your back.

t_en: I knew I could count on you, {F#sis}{M#bro}!

t_en: Origami bird... Hey, little birdie...

t_en: Stop tweeting! This is a full-blown emergency! Lives are on the line!!!

t_ch: 啾啾，啾啾啾——！
t_en: Tweet tweet, tweet tweet tweet!

t_en: No time to explain! That arcade machine is yours, right? Quick — let me squeeze in a couple rounds or we're toast!

t_ch: …啾？啾！啾啾——

t_en: Shangshang, I'll admit it, this Ghost Bird's got some skills.

t_en: Well, looks like I'll have to postpone my plans of shifting into a game streamer...

t_en: Little Gui, you should give that up entirely instead.

t_en: Tsk tsk tsk, winning a couple rounds doesn't mean you get to act smug. I doubt you'll stay on top for long.

t_ch: 光是打赢它也没用，它会赖皮。
t_en: Just winning won't cut it. That thing plays dirty.

t_en: Thank you so much, my dearest friends. You really saved the day!

t_en: Now that everything's settled, how about we take a stroll around Golden Hour?

t_ch: …而{NICKNAME}，等你回到罗浮，我也一定好好招待你的！
t_en: ...And {NICKNAME}, once you're back on the Luofu, I'll give you a proper welcome too!

t_en: Wait, what the heck!? You can talk!?

t_en: *Some truly fowl language*

t_en: Lancer. Sure, we've had some not-so-great memories, but we've fought together too. Doesn't that count for something?

t_en: Forgetting me so easily is rather rude.

t_en: Theoretically, yes. The Holy Grail's form remains, but its core has merged with the nature of this dream world, what you call "memoria."

t_ch: 可那位螺丝咕姆不知道做了什么手脚，竟提炼出了仪式的一部分，将其制作成了特殊的拟似装置。

t_ch: 不得不说，这个世界，也有着了不得的魔术师啊。

t_en: Anyway, the modified Holy Grail still carries the original competition's logic, along with the lingering data of the Penaconian Twelve Hours.

t_en: As long as... you "connect"? It's still hard to get used to the modern terminology. Anyway, as long as you connect to the Holy Grail, you can relive the Penaconian Holy Grail War in this dream bubble.

t_ch: 按他的说法，它将以使用者的记忆为媒介，载入命途行者的模拟数据。
t_en: According to him, it will fill in the simulation data of the Pathstriders using the user's memory as a medium.

t_ch: 你或许能在「圣杯」里遇上不少老朋友，但我对此实在是没什么兴趣。
t_en: You may meet many old friends in the Holy Grail, but I'm honestly not interested.

t_ch: 英雄们斗技游戏的{RUBY_B#英灵殿}瓦尔哈拉{RUBY_E#}？我还不想那么早去那儿呢。不过，确实会比没有纷争的{RUBY_B#Meg Mell}常乐原野{RUBY_E#}更适合我也说不定。
t_en: The heroes' arena in {RUBY_B#the Temple of Heroes}Valhalla{RUBY_E#}? Not ready to visit there just yet. Though I suppose it might fit me more than the peaceful {RUBY_B#Meg Mell}Elysian Fields{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 好了，螺丝咕姆先生让我传的话我已经传到了，该继续去外面挥霍剩余的时间了。
t_en: Anyway, I've delivered Mr. Screwllum's message. It's time to waste the rest of my days outside.

t_ch: 至于剩下的东西，用你自己的眼睛去看吧。
t_en: As for the rest, go and take a look for yourself.

t_en: (He's quick to run. Forget it. I'll check out what's changed in the modified Holy Grail.)

t_ch: 呦，最近过得如何？
t_en: So, how's life treating you these days?

t_ch: 不得不说，匹诺康尼可真是个好地方，尤其是这儿的酒吧。就连波提欧兄弟，也对这儿的口味赞不绝口。
t_en: I've got to say, Penacony is quite the place, especially the bar. Even my bro Boothill's been raving about the drinks.

t_en: Where did you get the money from?

t_en: Don't give me that look. I won a championship at the Roboball Contest and helped the Bloodhound Family clear out the monsters on the Nightmare's borders.

t_ch: 我不会给你搭线的。

t_ch: 也就是朋友关系。
t_en: Just friends.

t_ch: 呵呵。
